summary: Linked `src/melee/gr/grfzerocar.c` by moving it from NonMatching to Matching, updating GALE01 symbols, and applying small codegen/data-ordering fixes around F-Zero car stage data, `grFZeroCar_801CAFBC`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2`. The PR used an explicit `"archive"` assert string, a dummy `fakeFunc(Vec3)` workaround for `.sdata2` constant ordering, local layout/codegen adjustments, and an expanded scale-multiply block. decomp.dev reported 4 new matches, including 100% for `main/melee/gr/grfzerocar` `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, `.data`, and `grFZeroCar_801CAFBC`.

key_files.role: Source changes to make the F-Zero car ground/stage object match and link.
Updated `.rodata`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` labels, scopes, and object sizes for grFZeroCar data.

what_changed: Removed placeholder/platform and unused baselib includes from `grfzerocar.c`, keeping the needed ground, anime, datfiles, lb, sc, mtx, and jobj includes.
Added `static char grFZeroCar_804D4598[8] = "archive";` and changed `HSD_ASSERT(95, archive)` to `HSD_ASSERTMSG(95, archive, grFZeroCar_804D4598)`.
Added a dummy `static void fakeFunc(Vec3)` that assigns `0.0f` and `1.0` values, explicitly documented in the PR as being for `.sdata2` ordering.
Adjusted `setup_car_child` pointer arithmetic from `(u8*) archive->unk4->unk8 + offset` to `(u8*) offset + (uintptr_t) archive->unk4->unk8`.
In `grFZeroCar_801CAFBC`, reordered locals, added six `u32 pad` locals, and changed `root = GET_JOBJ(gobj)` to `root = gobj->hsd_obj`.
Replaced `multiplyScale(tra_jobj, ground_scale)` with an explicit block using `HSD_JObjGetScale`, multiplying `scl.x/y/z`, then `HSD_JObjSetScale`.

Updated symbol metadata: `grFZeroCar_803E0BD8` size changed from `0x188` to `0x168`, local string symbols `@403` and `@404` were inserted before `grIz_803E0D60`, and multiple grFZeroCar small-data symbols were converted to local `@NNN` labels.
Added local section labels such as `...rodata.0` and `...data.0` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
smart_moves: Used `HSD_ASSERTMSG` with a named `"archive"` string to materialize the expected assert-message data instead of relying on a bare `HSD_ASSERT`.
Kept the `.sdata2` ordering workaround localized and explicitly commented as a fake function, making the matching hack easy to find and revisit.
Expanded `multiplyScale` into the exact `HSD_JObjGetScale`/component multiply/`HSD_JObjSetScale` sequence, avoiding helper abstraction when it prevented full function matching.
Updated symbol scopes to local `@NNN` names where the diff indicates the strings/constants were not true global `grFZeroCar_` symbols.
Changed the build configuration only after the object had enough source and data fixes to be treated as Matching; the bot report confirms the object sections reached 100% for the reported matches.
decomp_lessons: Linking a stage object can require finishing small data and symbol-map details, not just matching C control flow.

For `.sdata` and `.sdata2`, symbol order and scope are part of the match. Use local `@NNN` labels for compiler-local constants/strings when the evidence supports it, rather than over-naming everything as module globals.
Assertion macro choice affects emitted data. Switching from `HSD_ASSERT` to `HSD_ASSERTMSG` can be necessary when the original binary contains an assert string such as `"archive"`.

Dummy static functions can be used as a last-resort ordering shim for small-data constants, but this PR's author and reviewer both treated ordering as uncertain; future work should prefer a cleaner ordering tactic if one is known.
Equivalent-looking C expressions can affect generated code. This PR used unusual pointer arithmetic and direct `gobj->hsd_obj` access to satisfy matching constraints.
Inlining a helper by hand can recover remaining bytes when helper use changes codegen; here, replacing `multiplyScale` with explicit JObj scale get/mutate/set helped finish `grFZeroCar_801CAFBC`.
naming_conventions: Module globals use the `grFZeroCar_<address>` pattern, e.g. `grFZeroCar_803E0BD8`, `grFZeroCar_803B7E50`, and `grFZeroCar_804D4598`.
Compiler-local data and constants in `symbols.txt` use `@NNN` labels, e.g. `@401`, `@402`, `@405`, `@142`, `@143`, `@406`, `@407`, and `@408`.
Section-local labels use names like `...rodata.0` and `...data.0`.
The local struct name `grFZeroCarEntry` follows the module name plus logical role pattern.
The assert-message string was represented as `static char grFZeroCar_804D4598[8] = "archive";`, matching the address-based module global naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a commented fake static function that references `Vec3`, `0.0f`, and `1.0` to force `.sdata2` float/double ordering when necessary.
Try `HSD_ASSERTMSG` instead of `HSD_ASSERT` when a string must appear in `.sdata` at the correct point.
Direct field access such as `gobj->hsd_obj` may match differently than a macro such as `GET_JOBJ(gobj)`.
Dummy padding locals and local-variable declaration order can be used to influence stack/register layout.
Manual expansion of helper behavior can match the original instruction sequence better than a helper call.
Pointer arithmetic written in an unusual but equivalent form can affect compiler output; preserve only when matching evidence supports it.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.body: The author was unsure how to do `.sdata2` ordering properly and used a `fakeFunc(Vec3)` workaround found from an earlier message.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.body: Reviewer asked `@PsiLupan @r-burns` how to fix string ordering, noting they tried inlines and pragmas but could not get it to behave.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.body: One review was CHANGES_REQUESTED with no body, followed by an APPROVED review with no body. The dump does not include a documented final explanation for the string-ordering issue.

follow_up_queries: string ordering inlines pragmas grfzerocar ribbanya
